description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage. The cannon barrel is made of dark, heavy metal with ornate engravings and reinforced rings along its length. The carriage is constructed from thick, weathered wooden planks, featuring metal brackets and bolts for structural support. Four solid metal-rimmed wheels are attached to the sides, allowing the cannon to be moved short distances. The front and rear views reveal the bore of the cannon and the robust wooden frame, while the side views highlight the mounting hardware and axle details. The overall appearance suggests a robust, functional design typical of artillery used in historical urban fortifications or military parades.
description: A heavy, ornate metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four metal-rimmed wheels and detailed hardware.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cannon's muzzle, reinforced barrel rings, and the symmetrical wooden carriage structure.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full length of the cannon barrel, wooden carriage, and two large metal-rimmed wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the breech of the cannon and the back of the wooden carriage, with visible wheel axles.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cannon's length, carriage details, and wheel construction.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the structure consists of exposed metal and wood with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid black, brown, and silver with no camouflage pattern.
